modules.moduleComment:
CSS class generation utilities.

Produces CSS output from class definitions, handles interpretation of
dynamic classes, and provides collection management for extracted classes.

declarations.name: generate_classes
declarations.docComment:
Generates CSS class declarations from templates.
Supports up to 3 dimensions of multiplicative combinations.
declarations.typeSignature: <T1 = string, T2 = string, T3 = string>(template: ClassTemplateFn<T1, T2, T3>, values: Iterable<T1>, secondary?: Iterable<T2> | undefined, tertiary?: Iterable<...> | undefined): Record<...>
examples:
```ts
// Simple list
generate_classes(
  v => ({ name: `position_${v}`, css: `position: ${v};` }),
  ['static', 'relative', 'absolute']
)
```

```ts
// Two dimensions (multiplicative)
generate_classes(
  (dir, size) => ({ name: `m${dir}_${size}`, css: `margin-${dir}: ${size};` }),
  ['top', 'bottom'],
  ['0', '1px', '2px']
)
```

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: function that generates CSS from values, can return null to skip

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: primary iterable of values

parameters.name: secondary
parameters.description: optional second dimension (makes it multiplicative)

parameters.name: tertiary
parameters.description: optional third dimension for even more combinations

declarations.name: format_spacing_value
declarations.docComment:
Format spacing values for CSS (handles 0, auto, percentages, pixels, and CSS variables).
Used by margin, padding, gap, inset, top/right/bottom/left, etc.

declarations.name: format_dimension_value
declarations.docComment:
Format width/height values for CSS (handles 0, auto, percentages, pixels, content values, and CSS variables).
Used by width and height properties.

declarations.name: generate_property_classes
declarations.docComment: Generate classes for a single CSS property with various values.
declarations.typeSignature: (property: string, values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((value: string) => string) | undefined, prefix?: string): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: the CSS property name (e.g. 'font-size', 'gap')

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

parameters.name: formatter
parameters.description: optional function to format values (e.g. v => `var(--space_${v})`)

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: optional class name prefix (defaults to property with dashes replaced by underscores)

declarations.name: generate_directional_classes
declarations.docComment:
Generate directional classes for properties like margin and padding.
Creates classes for all directions: base, top, right, bottom, left, x (horizontal), y (vertical).
declarations.typeSignature: (property: string, values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((v: string) => string) | undefined): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: the base CSS property name (e.g. 'margin', 'padding')

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

parameters.name: formatter
parameters.description: optional function to format values (defaults to identity)

declarations.name: generate_border_radius_corners
declarations.docComment:
Generate border radius corner classes for all four corners.
Creates classes for top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right corners.
declarations.typeSignature: (values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((value: string) => string) | undefined): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

parameters.name: formatter
parameters.description: optional function to format values

declarations.name: generate_shadow_classes
declarations.docComment:
Generate shadow classes for various shadow types and sizes.
Creates classes for regular, top, bottom, inset, inset-top, and inset-bottom shadows.
Each shadow uses color-mix with alpha values for transparency.
declarations.typeSignature: (sizes: Iterable<string>, alpha_mapping: Record<string, string>): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: sizes
parameters.description: the shadow size variants (xs, sm, md, lg, xl)

parameters.name: alpha_mapping
parameters.description: mapping of sizes to alpha numbers (1-5)

declarations.name: modified_class_interpreter
declarations.docComment:
Interpreter for modified token/composite classes (e.g., `hover:p_md`, `md:box`, `dark:hover:panel`).
Applies modifiers to existing declaration-based or ruleset-based classes.

This interpreter must run BEFORE `css_literal_interpreter` to handle cases like `hover:box`
where `box` is a known class (not a CSS property).

declarations.name: css_literal_interpreter
declarations.docComment:
Interpreter for CSS-literal classes (e.g., `display:flex`, `hover:opacity:80%`).
Generates full CSS rulesets including any modifier wrappers.

declarations.name: css_class_interpreters
declarations.docComment:
Collection of all builtin interpreters for dynamic CSS class generation.
Order matters: `modified_class_interpreter` runs first to handle `hover:box` before
`css_literal_interpreter` tries to interpret it as `hover:box` (property:value).

declarations.name: ResolveComposesResult
declarations.docComment: Result from resolving a `composes` array to combined declarations.

declarations.name: resolve_class_definition
declarations.docComment:
Resolves a class definition's declaration, handling `composes` composition.

If the definition has a `composes` property, resolves those classes recursively
and combines with any explicit `declaration`. If no `composes`, returns the
explicit `declaration` directly.
declarations.typeSignature: (def: CssClassDefinitionStatic, class_name: string, definitions: Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined>, css_properties?: Set<...> | null): ResolveComposesResult

parameters.name: def
parameters.description: the class definition to resolve

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: the name of the class being resolved (for error messages)

parameters.name: definitions
parameters.description: record of all known class definitions

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description: set of valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip

declarations.returnDescription: combined declaration or an error

declarations.name: resolve_composes
declarations.docComment:
Resolves an array of class names to their combined CSS declarations.

Recursively resolves nested `composes` arrays and combines all declarations.
Validates that referenced classes exist and are resolvable (not rulesets or interpreters).
Supports unmodified CSS literals (e.g., `text-align:center`) in the composes array.

Deduplication behavior:
- Diamond dependencies (class reached via different composition branches) are silently skipped
- Redundant listings (class already included by an earlier sibling in this array) emit a warning
declarations.typeSignature: (class_names: string[], definitions: Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined>, resolution_stack: Set<string>, visited: Set<string>, original_class_name: string, css_properties?: Set<...> | null): ResolveComposesResult

parameters.name: class_names
parameters.description: array of class names to resolve

parameters.name: definitions
parameters.description: record of all known class definitions

parameters.name: resolution_stack
parameters.description: set of class names currently being resolved (for cycle detection)

parameters.name: visited
parameters.description: set of all class names already resolved (for deduplication)

parameters.name: original_class_name
parameters.description: the class name being defined (for error messages)

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description: set of valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip

declarations.returnDescription: combined declarations or an error

modules.moduleComment:
CSS class resolution utilities for composing class definitions.

Provides the `resolve_composes` helper to recursively resolve class names
to their combined CSS declarations.

declarations.name: CssClasses
declarations.docComment:
Collection of CSS classes extracted from source files.
Tracks classes per-file for efficient incremental updates.
Handles include/exclude filtering and explicit class tracking.
Uses `null` instead of empty collections to avoid allocation overhead.

members.name: constructor
members.docComment: Creates a new `CssClasses` collection.
members.typeSignature: (additional_classes?: Set<string> | null, exclude_classes?: Set<string> | null): CssClasses

parameters.name: additional_classes
parameters.description: classes to always include (also treated as explicit for warnings)

parameters.name: exclude_classes
parameters.description: classes to exclude from output (also suppresses warnings)

members.name: add
members.docComment:
Adds extraction results for a file.
Replaces any previous classes and diagnostics for this file.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: file identifier

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: extraction data fields to store for this file

members.name: get
members.docComment: Gets all unique class names as a Set (with exclude filter applied).

members.name: get_with_locations
members.docComment:
Gets all classes with their source locations (with exclude filter applied).
Locations from `additional_classes` are null.

members.name: get_all
members.docComment:
Gets all classes and their locations in a single call.
More efficient than calling `get()` and `get_with_locations()` separately
when both are needed (avoids potential double recalculation).

Results have exclude filter applied and `explicit_classes` includes `additional_classes`.
members.typeSignature: (): { all_classes: Set<string>; all_classes_with_locations: Map<string, SourceLocation[] | null>; explicit_classes: Set<string> | null; all_elements: Set<...>; explicit_elements: Set<...> | null; explicit_variables: Set<...> | null; }
members.returnType: { all_classes: Set<string>; all_classes_with_locations: Map<string, SourceLocation[] | null>; explicit_classes: Set<string> | null; all_elements: Set<...>; explicit_elements: Set<...> | null; explicit_variables: Set<...> | null; }

members.name: get_diagnostics
members.docComment: Gets all extraction diagnostics from all files.

Collection management for extracted CSS classes.

Uses `null` instead of empty collections to avoid allocation overhead.

declarations.name: CssLiteralParseResult
declarations.docComment:
Result of parsing a CSS-literal class name.

Uses a discriminated union (Result type) because parsing a single class
is binary: it either succeeds or fails entirely. This differs from
`ExtractionResult` which uses embedded diagnostics because file
extraction can partially succeed (some classes extracted, others have errors).

Uses `| null` for diagnostics to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.diagnostics) { ... }`

Extracts colon-separated segments from a class name, handling parentheses.
Parenthesized content (like function arguments) is kept intact.

```ts

extract_segments('nth-child(2n+1):color:red') // → ['nth-child(2n+1)', 'color', 'red']
```

modules.moduleComment:
CSS-literal syntax parser, validator, and interpreter.

Enables writing utility classes using actual CSS syntax:

- `display:flex` → `.display\:flex { display: flex; }`
- `hover:opacity:80%` → `.hover\:opacity\:80\%:hover { opacity: 80%; }`
- `md:dark:hover:before:opacity:80%` → nested CSS with media query, ancestor, state, pseudo-element

@see {@link https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_css} for documentation

declarations.name: CssExtractionOptions
declarations.docComment:
Options for CSS class extraction from source files.
Controls which files to scan and how to parse them.

members.name: filter_file
members.docComment:
Filter function to determine which files to extract classes from.
By default, extracts from .svelte, .html, .ts, .js, .tsx, .jsx files,
excluding test files and .gen files.

members.name: acorn_plugins
members.docComment:
Additional acorn plugins for parsing.
Use `acorn-jsx` for React/Preact/Solid projects.

declarations.name: CssClassOptions
declarations.docComment:
Options for CSS class definitions and interpretation.
Controls how classes are defined and how dynamic classes are generated.

members.name: class_definitions
members.docComment:
Additional class definitions to merge with defaults.
User definitions take precedence over defaults with the same name.
Required when `include_default_classes` is `false`.

members.name: include_default_classes
members.docComment:
Whether to include default class definitions (token and composite classes).
When `false`, `class_definitions` is required.

members.name: class_interpreters
members.docComment:
Custom interpreters for dynamic class generation.
Replaces the builtin interpreters entirely if provided.

declarations.name: BaseCssOption
declarations.docComment:
Type for the base_css option used by CSS generators.

Supports four forms:
- `undefined` - Use default `style.css` (framework defaults)
- `null` - Disable base styles entirely (explicit opt-out)
- `string` - Custom CSS to replace defaults
- `(default_css) => string` - Callback to modify default CSS

See module documentation for the `undefined` vs `null` convention.

declarations.name: VariablesOption
declarations.docComment:
Type for the variables option used by CSS generators.

Supports four forms:
- `undefined` - Use default variables (framework defaults)
- `null` - Disable theme generation entirely (explicit opt-out)
- `Array<StyleVariable>` - Custom variables array (replaces defaults)
- `(defaults) => Array<StyleVariable>` - Callback to modify defaults

See module documentation for the `undefined` vs `null` convention.

declarations.name: CssOutputOptions
declarations.docComment:
Options for CSS output generation (theme + base + utilities).
Controls how the three CSS layers are combined.

members.name: base_css
members.docComment:
Base styles (element defaults) configuration.
- `undefined` (default): Use default style.css
- `null`: Disable base styles entirely
- `string`: Custom CSS to replace defaults
- `(default_css) => string`: Callback to modify default CSS

members.name: variables
members.docComment:
Theme variables configuration.
- `undefined` (default): Use default variables from fuz_css
- `null`: Disable theme entirely
- `Array<StyleVariable>`: Custom variable definitions (replaces defaults)
- `(defaults) => Array<StyleVariable>`: Callback to modify default variables

members.name: theme_specificity
members.docComment:
Specificity multiplier for theme CSS selectors.
Defaults to 1 which generates `:root`, higher values generate more specific selectors (e.g., `:root:root`).

members.name: additional_classes
members.docComment:
Classes to always include in the output, regardless of detection.
Useful for dynamically generated class names that can't be statically extracted.

members.name: additional_elements
members.docComment:
Additional HTML elements to always include base styles for.
Use `'all'` to include all base styles regardless of detection.
Useful for elements generated at runtime via `document.createElement()`.

members.name: additional_variables
members.docComment:
Additional CSS variables to always include in theme output.
Use `'all'` to include all theme variables regardless of detection.
Useful for variables referenced dynamically.

members.name: exclude_classes
members.docComment:
Classes to exclude from the output, even if detected.
Useful for filtering out false positives from extraction.

members.name: exclude_elements
members.docComment:
Elements to exclude from base CSS output, even if detected.
Useful for filtering out elements you don't want styles for.

members.name: exclude_variables
members.docComment:
CSS variables to exclude from theme output, even if referenced.
Useful for filtering out variables you don't want in the theme.

declarations.name: CssDiagnosticsOptions
declarations.docComment: Options for error and warning handling.

members.name: on_error
members.docComment:
How to handle CSS-literal errors during generation.
- 'log': Log errors, skip invalid classes, continue
- 'throw': Throw on first error, fail the build

members.name: on_warning
members.docComment:
How to handle warnings during generation.
- 'log': Log warnings, continue
- 'throw': Throw on first warning, fail the build
- 'ignore': Suppress warnings entirely

declarations.name: CssCacheOptions
declarations.docComment: Options for cache behavior.

members.name: cache_dir
members.docComment: Cache directory relative to project root.

members.name: deps
members.docComment:
Filesystem deps for cache management.
Defaults to production implementation. Override for testing.

declarations.name: CssGeneratorBaseOptions
declarations.docComment:
Combined base options shared by both Gro and Vite plugins.
These options work identically in both contexts.

modules.moduleComment:
Shared options for CSS generation plugins (Gro and Vite).

Both `gen_fuz_css` (Gro generator) and `vite_plugin_fuz_css` share
the same core options for extraction, generation, and bundled CSS.
This module provides the shared types to ensure consistency.

## Bundled mode (default)

By default, the generated CSS (`virtual:fuz.css` or `./fuz.css`) includes
only the content your code uses from all three layers:
- Base styles (element defaults)
- Theme variables (CSS custom properties)
- Utility classes

## `undefined` vs `null` Convention

Configuration options that accept both `undefined` and `null` follow this pattern:

- **`undefined`** - Use framework defaults. The feature is enabled with standard behavior.
- **`null`** - Explicitly disable the feature. No output is generated for that layer.

This applies to `BaseCssOption` and `VariablesOption`.
Setting both to `null` enables "utility-only mode" where you manage
your own theme and base styles via direct imports (`@fuzdev/fuz_css/style.css`
and `theme.css`, which include all content).

declarations.name: is_single_selector_ruleset
declarations.docComment:
Checks if a ruleset has only a single simple selector (just the class name).
Used to detect rulesets that could be converted to declaration format.

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: parsed rules from the ruleset

parameters.name: escaped_class_name
parameters.description: the CSS-escaped class name (e.g., "box" or "hover\\:card")

declarations.returnDescription: true if there's exactly one rule with selector ".class_name"

declarations.name: ruleset_contains_class
declarations.docComment:
Checks if any selector in the ruleset contains the expected class name.
Used to validate that ruleset definitions match their key.

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: parsed rules from the ruleset

parameters.name: escaped_class_name
parameters.description: the CSS-escaped class name (e.g., "clickable" or "hover\\:card")

declarations.returnDescription: true if at least one selector contains ".class_name"

declarations.name: extract_css_comment
declarations.docComment:
Extracts the CSS comment from a ruleset (if any).
Looks for comments before the first rule.

parameters.name: css
parameters.description: raw CSS string

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: parsed rules

declarations.returnDescription: comment text without delimiters, or null if no comment

declarations.name: SkippedModifierInfo
declarations.docComment:
Information about a modifier that was skipped for a selector during ruleset modification.
The selector is still included in output, just without the conflicting modifier applied.

members.name: selector
members.docComment: The specific selector where the modifier was skipped (not the full selector list)

members.name: reason
members.docComment: Reason the modifier was skipped

members.name: conflicting_modifier
members.docComment: The conflicting modifier that was not applied (e.g., "::before" or ":hover")

declarations.name: ModifiedSelectorGroupResult
declarations.docComment:
Result from modifying a selector group with conflict detection.

Uses `| null` for skipped_modifiers to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.skipped_modifiers) { ... }`

members.name: selector
members.docComment: The modified selector list as a string

members.name: skipped_modifiers
members.docComment: Information about modifiers skipped for specific selectors, or null if none

declarations.name: ModifiedRulesetResult
declarations.docComment:
Result from generating a modified ruleset.

Uses `| null` for skipped_modifiers to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.skipped_modifiers) { ... }`

members.name: css
members.docComment: The generated CSS

members.name: skipped_modifiers
members.docComment: Information about modifiers that were skipped for certain rules, or null if none

declarations.name: split_selector_list
declarations.docComment: Splits a selector list by commas, respecting parentheses, brackets, and quoted strings.
examples:
```ts
split_selector_list('.a, .b') // → ['.a', '.b']
split_selector_list('.a:not(.b), .c') // → ['.a:not(.b)', '.c']
split_selector_list('.a[data-x="a,b"], .c') // → ['.a[data-x="a,b"]', '.c']
```

declarations.name: find_compound_end
declarations.docComment:
Finds the end position of the compound selector containing the class at class_pos.
A compound selector is a sequence of simple selectors without combinators.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: the CSS selector string

parameters.name: class_pos
parameters.description: position of the `.` in `.class_name`

declarations.returnDescription: position where state modifiers should be inserted (before any pseudo-element)

declarations.name: modify_single_selector
declarations.docComment: Modifies a single CSS selector to add modifiers.
declarations.typeSignature: (selector: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, state_css: string, pseudo_element_css: string): string

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: a single CSS selector (not a selector list)

parameters.name: original_class
parameters.description: the base class name (e.g., "menuitem")

parameters.name: new_class_escaped
parameters.description: the escaped new class name (e.g., "hover\\:menuitem")

parameters.name: state_css
parameters.description: state modifier CSS to insert (e.g., ":hover")

parameters.name: pseudo_element_css
parameters.description: pseudo-element modifier CSS to insert (e.g., "::before")

declarations.returnDescription: modified selector

declarations.name: modify_selector_group
declarations.docComment:
Modifies a selector list (comma-separated selectors) to add modifiers.
Handles conflicts per-selector: if one selector in a list has a conflict,
only that selector skips the modifier; other selectors still get it.
declarations.typeSignature: (selector_group: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, states_to_add: string[], pseudo_element_css: string): ModifiedSelectorGroupResult

parameters.name: selector_group
parameters.description: CSS selector list (may contain commas)

parameters.name: original_class
parameters.description: the base class name

parameters.name: new_class_escaped
parameters.description: the escaped new class name

parameters.name: states_to_add
parameters.description: individual state modifiers (e.g., [":hover", ":focus"])

parameters.name: pseudo_element_css
parameters.description: pseudo-element modifier CSS to insert (e.g., "::before")

declarations.returnDescription: result with modified selector list and information about skipped modifiers

declarations.name: generate_modified_ruleset
declarations.docComment:
Generates CSS for a modified ruleset with applied modifiers.

Conflict handling is per-selector within selector lists:
- For `.plain:hover, .plain:active` with `hover:` modifier, only `.plain:hover` skips
  the `:hover` addition; `.plain:active` still gets `:hover` appended.
- For multiple states like `:hover:focus`, each is checked individually; conflicting
  states are skipped while non-conflicting ones are still applied.
declarations.typeSignature: (original_ruleset: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, state_css: string, pseudo_element_css: string, media_wrapper: string | null, ancestor_wrapper: string | null): ModifiedRulesetResult

parameters.name: original_ruleset
parameters.description: the original CSS ruleset string

parameters.name: original_class
parameters.description: the base class name

parameters.name: new_class_escaped
parameters.description: the escaped new class name with modifiers

parameters.name: state_css
parameters.description: state modifier CSS (e.g., ":hover" or ":hover:focus")

parameters.name: pseudo_element_css
parameters.description: pseudo-element modifier CSS (e.g., "::before")

parameters.name: media_wrapper
parameters.description: media query wrapper (e.g., "@media (width >= 48rem)")

parameters.name: ancestor_wrapper
parameters.description: ancestor wrapper (e.g., ":root.dark")

declarations.returnDescription: result with generated CSS and information about skipped modifiers

modules.moduleComment:
CSS ruleset parser using Svelte's CSS parser.

Parses CSS rulesets to extract selectors and declarations with position information.
Used for:
- Phase 0a: Detecting single-selector rulesets that could be converted to declaration format
- Phase 2: Modifying selectors for modifier support on composite classes

declarations.name: extract_css_variables
declarations.docComment:
Extracts CSS variable names from a CSS string.

Parses `var(--name)` patterns and returns the variable names
without the `--` prefix. Handles fallback values by extracting
only the primary variable reference.

parameters.name: css
parameters.description: CSS string potentially containing `var(--*)` references

declarations.returnDescription: set of variable names (without `--` prefix)

declarations.name: has_css_variables
declarations.docComment:
Checks if a CSS string contains any CSS variable references.

More efficient than `extract_css_variables` when you only need
to know if variables exist, not what they are.

parameters.name: css
parameters.description: CSS string to check

declarations.returnDescription: true if the string contains `var(--*)` patterns

modules.moduleComment:
CSS variable extraction utilities.

Provides shared helper functions for extracting CSS custom property references
from CSS strings. Used by style_rule_parser, variable_graph, class_variable_index,
and css_class_generation.

declarations.name: default_cache_deps

modules.moduleComment:
Production implementations of deps interfaces.

Provides real filesystem deps for production use.
For interface definitions and dependency injection pattern, see `deps.ts`.

declarations.name: CacheDeps
declarations.docComment:
Cache-related file system deps.
Abstracted to enable test isolation from the actual filesystem.

Named `CacheDeps` (not `FsDeps`) because it only covers
the specific deps needed for cache management, not general
filesystem access.

Reads a text file.

members.name: write_text_atomic
members.docComment:
Writes a text file atomically (temp file + rename for crash safety).
Creates parent directories if they don't exist.

members.name: unlink
members.docComment:
Removes a file. Returns a `not_found` error if the file doesn't exist —
callers that want `rm -f` semantics should ignore that kind explicitly.

members.name: column

Diagnostic from CSS class interpretation.
Used internally by interpreters; converted to `GenerationDiagnostic` with locations.

Error thrown when CSS generation encounters errors or warnings

Contains the full diagnostics array for programmatic access.

modules.moduleComment:
Diagnostic types for CSS class extraction and generation.

Provides a unified diagnostic system across all phases:
- Extraction: Parsing source files to find class names
- Generation: Producing CSS output from class definitions

Comment hint - extracted via `@fuz-classes`, NOT via variable name pattern.
Variable name `fromComment` doesn't end in class/classes/className.

modules.moduleComment:
Example CSS class exports demonstrating node_modules extraction.

This module exists to verify that the Vite plugin extracts classes from
dependencies in node_modules. The exports test specific extraction patterns
that require being in a separate module.

**Important:** Variable names must match `CLASS_NAME_PATTERN` in `css_class_extractor.ts`.
Supported suffixes: `class`, `classes`, `className`, `classNames`, `classList`, `classLists`
(also snake_case variants like `class_name`, `class_names`, `class_list`, `class_lists`).

**Patterns demonstrated:**
- Naming patterns: All `CLASS_NAME_PATTERN` suffix variants
- Expression patterns: Ternary, logical AND, arrays
- Comment hints: `@fuz-classes` directive

Token, composite, and literal classes are demonstrated inline in the examples
since they don't require special extraction testing.

members.name: content
members.docComment: Source file contents.

members.name: content_hash
members.docComment: Content hash to validate the cache against.

members.name: cache_path
members.docComment: Cache file path, or null to skip the cache (CI or caching disabled).

members.name: filename
members.docComment: File path, for parser selection and diagnostic locations.

members.name: from_cache
members.docComment: Whether the extraction came from cache.

members.name: cache_path_to_write
members.docComment: Cache file to write (miss with caching enabled), or null.

declarations.name: extract_file_cached
declarations.docComment:
Loads the cached extraction when its content hash matches, otherwise extracts
fresh. On a miss with caching enabled, `cache_path_to_write` carries the path
the caller should write.

modules.moduleComment:
Cache-aware single-file class extraction shared by the Gro generator and the
Vite plugin. Returns extraction sourced from cache when the content hash
matches, else freshly parsed, plus the cache path to write on a miss.

Callers own everything around this — in-memory hash short-circuits, the Vite
deletion-race guard, diagnostic logging, stats counting, and how/when to
write the cache (fire-and-forget vs. batched).

declarations.name: FileFilter
declarations.docComment: Filter function to determine which files to process for CSS class extraction.

declarations.name: filter_file_default
declarations.docComment:
Default file filter for CSS class extraction.
Includes .svelte, .html, .ts, .js, .tsx, .jsx files.
Excludes test files (.test.ts, .spec.ts) and generated files (.gen.ts).
Excludes files in test directories (`/test/`, `/tests/`, `/__tests__/`, `/__mocks__/`).

modules.moduleComment: File filtering utilities for CSS class extraction.

declarations.name: GenFuzCssOptions
declarations.docComment:
Options for the Gro CSS generator.
Extends the shared base options with Gro-specific settings.

members.name: include_stats
members.docComment: Whether to include file and resolution statistics in the output.

members.name: project_root
members.docComment: Project root directory. Source paths must be under this directory.

members.name: concurrency
members.docComment:
Max concurrent file processing (cache read + extract).
Bottlenecked by CPU-bound AST parsing.

members.name: cache_io_concurrency
members.docComment: Max concurrent cache writes and deletes (I/O-bound).

modules.moduleComment:
[Gro generator](https://github.com/fuzdev/gro) for creating optimized utility CSS from extracted class names.
Scans source files, extracts CSS classes with AST-based parsing, and generates
only the CSS for classes actually used. Includes per-file caching with content
hash validation for fast incremental rebuilds.

declarations.name: GenerateCssOptions
declarations.docComment:
Inputs to `generate_css`. The first group mirrors the shape returned by
`CssClasses.get_all()` plus its diagnostics, so callers can forward it
directly.

members.name: all_classes
members.docComment: All detected class names, already exclude-filtered.

members.name: all_classes_with_locations
members.docComment: Source locations per class, for diagnostics.

members.name: explicit_classes
members.docComment: Classes from `@fuz-classes`/`additional_classes`; unresolved ones error.

members.name: all_elements
members.docComment: All detected HTML element names.

members.name: explicit_elements
members.docComment: Elements from `@fuz-elements`; unresolved ones error.

members.name: explicit_variables
members.docComment: Variables from `@fuz-variables`; unresolved ones error.

members.name: extraction_diagnostics
members.docComment: Diagnostics accumulated during extraction.

members.name: detected_css_variables
members.docComment:
CSS variables referenced in source, already filtered to known theme
variables by the caller. `@fuz-variables` are merged in here automatically.

members.name: css_properties
members.docComment: Valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip.

members.name: resources
members.docComment: Bundled resources, or null for utility-only mode.

members.name: log
members.docComment: Optional logger; only used to emit resolution stats when `include_stats`.

members.name: include_stats
members.docComment: Whether to compute and log resolution statistics.

members.name: css
members.docComment: Final CSS without banner comments — callers add their own.

members.name: diagnostics
members.docComment: Extraction + generation + resolution diagnostics, unfiltered.

declarations.name: generate_css
declarations.docComment:
Runs the full CSS-generation pipeline: utility classes via
`generate_classes_css`, then — when base or theme output is enabled and
bundled `resources` are available — base styles and theme variables via
`resolve_css` + `generate_bundled_css`. Returns the combined CSS and every
diagnostic produced along the way.

modules.moduleComment:
Shared CSS-generation pipeline for the Gro generator and the Vite plugin.

Both consumers extract classes from source files their own way (batch via the
Gro filer, incrementally via Vite's transform hook), then funnel the
aggregated result through this single function so their CSS output stays
identical. Callers own banner wrapping and error/warning dispatch (those
differ by logger), this owns the generate → resolve → bundle pipeline.

declarations.name: ModifierType
declarations.docComment:
Type of modifier determining its position in the class name and CSS output.

Order in class names: `[media:][ancestor:][state...:][pseudo-element:]property:value`

declarations.name: ModifierDefinition
declarations.docComment: Definition for a single modifier.

members.name: name
members.docComment: The prefix used in class names (e.g., 'hover', 'md', 'dark')

members.name: type
members.docComment: Type determines position in modifier order and CSS output behavior

members.name: css
members.docComment: The CSS output - wrapper for media/ancestor, suffix for state/pseudo-element

members.name: order
members.docComment: Optional ordering within type (for breakpoints, sorted by this value)

declarations.name: MODIFIERS
declarations.docComment:
All modifier definitions in a single declarative structure.
Adding a new modifier requires only adding to this array.

declarations.name: ANCESTOR_MODIFIERS

declarations.name: ALL_MODIFIER_NAMES
declarations.docComment: All modifier names for quick lookup

declarations.docComment: Pattern for parameterized nth-child: `nth-child(2n+1):`

declarations.name: NTH_LAST_CHILD_PATTERN
declarations.docComment: Pattern for parameterized nth-last-child: `nth-last-child(2n+1):`

declarations.name: extract_balanced_parens
declarations.docComment:
Extracts content from balanced parentheses after a prefix.
Supports nested parens for calc(), clamp(), min(), max(), etc.
examples:
```ts
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(800px)", "min-width") // "800px"
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(calc(100vw - 20px))", "min-width") // "calc(100vw - 20px)"
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(calc(100vw)", "min-width") // null (unbalanced)
```

parameters.name: segment
parameters.description: the full segment to parse (e.g., "min-width(calc(100vw - 200px))")

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: the prefix before the opening paren (e.g., "min-width")

declarations.returnDescription: the content inside the balanced parens, or null if no match/unbalanced

declarations.name: parse_arbitrary_breakpoint
declarations.docComment:
Parses an arbitrary breakpoint modifier.
Supports nested parentheses for calc(), clamp(), min(), max(), var().
Requires value to start with a digit or known CSS function to catch obvious mistakes.
examples:
```ts
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(800px)") // "@media (width >= 800px)"
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(calc(100vw - 200px))") // "@media (width >= calc(100vw - 200px))"
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(px)") // null (invalid - no digit or function)
```
declarations.returnDescription: the CSS media query or null if not an arbitrary breakpoint

declarations.name: parse_parameterized_state
declarations.docComment: Parses a parameterized state modifier (nth-child, nth-last-child, nth-of-type, nth-last-of-type).
declarations.typeSignature: (segment: string): { name: string; css: string; type: "state"; } | null
declarations.returnType: { name: string; css: string; type: "state"; } | null
declarations.returnDescription: object with name (including parameter) and CSS, or null if not parameterized

declarations.name: get_modifier
declarations.docComment:
Gets the modifier definition for a segment.
Handles both static modifiers and dynamic patterns (arbitrary breakpoints, parameterized states).
declarations.returnDescription: the modifier definition or null if not a known modifier

declarations.name: get_all_modifier_names
declarations.docComment: Gets all modifier names for error message suggestions.

modules.moduleComment:
Declarative modifier definitions for CSS-literal syntax.

Modifiers enable responsive, state-based, and contextual styling:
- Media modifiers: `md:`, `print:`, `motion-safe:`
- Ancestor modifiers: `dark:`, `light:`
- State modifiers: `hover:`, `focus:`, `disabled:`
- Pseudo-element modifiers: `before:`, `after:`

@see {@link https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_css} for documentation

parameters.description: the `StyleRuleIndex`

parameters.name: included_indices
parameters.description: set of rule indices to include

declarations.returnDescription: set of variable names (without -- prefix)

modules.moduleComment:
Style.css rule parser for bundled CSS generation.

Parses the fuz_css `style.css` file into a structured index that maps
CSS rules to the HTML elements and classes they style. This enables
the bundled CSS generator to include only the rules needed for
elements actually used in the project.

members.name: specificity
members.docComment:
Repeats the theme selector to handle unpredictable head content insertion order.
Accepts any integer >= 1, defaults to 2.

declarations.name: render_theme_variable
declarations.typeSignature: (variable: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }, dark?: boolean, comments?: boolean): string

parameters.name: variable
parameters.type: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }

declarations.name: default_themes
declarations.docComment: These are super basic proof-of-concept themes.

declarations.name: NumericScaleVariant
declarations.docComment:
The standard numeric scale used across multiple variable families.
Provides 13 steps from 00 (surface/subtle) to 100 (contrast/bold).

declarations.name: NumericScaleWithExtremesVariant
declarations.docComment:
Numeric scale with min/max extremes for scales that need untinted endpoints.
Used by text and shade scales where min/max represent pure black/white values.

declarations.name: TextScaleVariant
declarations.docComment: Full text scale including min/max extremes for knockout text.

declarations.name: ShadeScaleVariant
declarations.docComment: Full shade scale including min/max extremes for untinted surfaces.

declarations.name: icon_sizes
declarations.typeSignature: { icon_size_xs: string; icon_size_sm: string; icon_size_md: string; icon_size_lg: string; icon_size_xl: string; icon_size_xl2: string; icon_size_xl3: string; }

declarations.name: Z_INDEX_MAX
declarations.docComment: Maximum value for CSS z-index property (32-bit signed integer max).

declarations.name: StyleVariableInfo
declarations.docComment: Information about a single style variable and its dependencies.

members.name: name
members.docComment: Variable name (without -- prefix)

members.name: light_deps
members.docComment: Variables referenced in the light value

members.name: dark_deps
members.docComment: Variables referenced in the dark value

members.name: light_css
members.docComment: The CSS value for light mode, or undefined if not defined

members.name: dark_css
members.docComment: The CSS value for dark mode, or undefined if not defined

declarations.name: VariableDependencyGraph
declarations.docComment: Dependency graph for style variables.

members.name: variables
members.docComment: Map from variable name to its info

members.name: content_hash
members.docComment: Content hash for cache invalidation

declarations.name: build_variable_graph
declarations.docComment: Builds a dependency graph from an array of style variables.
declarations.typeSignature: (variables: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[], content_hash: string): VariableDependencyGraph

parameters.name: variables
parameters.type: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]
parameters.description: array of `StyleVariable` objects

parameters.name: content_hash
parameters.description: hash of the source for cache invalidation

declarations.returnDescription: `VariableDependencyGraph`

declarations.name: ResolveVariablesResult
declarations.docComment: Result from transitive variable resolution.

members.name: variables
members.docComment: All resolved variable names

members.name: warnings
members.docComment: Warning messages for cycles

members.name: missing
members.docComment: Variable names that were requested but not found in the graph

declarations.name: resolve_variables_transitive
declarations.docComment:
Resolves variables transitively using DFS with cycle detection.
When a variable is requested, all variables it depends on are included.
Both light and dark dependencies are always resolved together.

parameters.name: graph
parameters.description: the variable dependency graph

parameters.name: initial_variables
parameters.description: initial set of variable names to resolve

declarations.returnDescription: `ResolveVariablesResult` with all transitive dependencies

declarations.name: generate_theme_css
declarations.docComment: Generates theme CSS for the resolved variables.
declarations.typeSignature: (graph: VariableDependencyGraph, resolved_variables: Set<string>, specificity?: number): { light_css: string; dark_css: string; }

parameters.name: graph
parameters.description: the variable dependency graph

parameters.name: resolved_variables
parameters.description: set of variable names to include

parameters.name: specificity
parameters.description: number of times to repeat the selector (default 1)

declarations.returnDescription: object with `light_css` and `dark_css` strings

declarations.name: get_all_variable_names
declarations.docComment: Gets all variable names from the graph.

parameters.name: graph
parameters.description: the variable dependency graph

declarations.returnDescription: set of all variable names in the graph

declarations.name: has_variable
declarations.docComment: Checks if a variable exists in the graph.

parameters.name: graph
parameters.description: the variable dependency graph

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: variable name to check (without -- prefix)

declarations.returnDescription: true if the variable exists in the graph

declarations.name: find_similar_variable
declarations.docComment:
Finds the most similar variable in the graph to the given name.
Returns null if no variable exceeds the similarity threshold.

parameters.name: graph
parameters.description: the variable dependency graph

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: the variable name to find similar matches for

declarations.returnDescription: the most similar variable name, or null if none are similar enough

declarations.name: resolve_variables_option
declarations.docComment: Resolves a variables option to a concrete array of style variables.
declarations.typeSignature: (variables: VariablesOption): { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: the variables option (undefined, null, array, or callback)

declarations.returnType: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]
declarations.returnDescription: resolved array of style variables, or empty array if null

declarations.name: build_variable_graph_from_options
declarations.docComment:
Builds a variable dependency graph from a variables option.
Handles all option forms: undefined (defaults), null (disabled), array, or callback.

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: the variables option from generator config

declarations.returnDescription: `VariableDependencyGraph` built from the resolved variables

modules.moduleComment:
Variable dependency graph for bundled CSS generation.

Builds a dependency graph from style variables to enable transitive
resolution of CSS custom properties. When a variable is needed,
all variables it depends on are also included.

declarations.name: StyleVariable
declarations.docComment:
Zod schema for validating `StyleVariable` objects.
Use `safeParse` for validation; the `StyleVariable` type is defined separately
to preserve the `Flavored` brand on `name`.
declarations.typeSignature: ZodObject<{ name: ZodString; light: ZodOptional<ZodString>; dark: ZodOptional<ZodString>; summary: ZodOptional<ZodString>; }, $strip>

declarations.name: create_context
declarations.docComment:
Wraps Svelte's `setContext` and `getContext` for better ergonomics.
When no value is set in the context,
`get` throws an error and `get_maybe` returns `undefined`.
If a `fallback` is provided, the `value` argument to `set` is optional
and `get_maybe` is omitted from the type.
declarations.typeSignature: <T>(fallback: () => T): { get: () => T; set: (value?: T | undefined) => T; }

overloads.returnType: { get: () => T; set: (value?: T | undefined) => T; }
overloads.docComment:
Wraps Svelte's `setContext` and `getContext` for better ergonomics.
When no value is set in the context,
`get` throws an error and `get_maybe` returns `undefined`.
If a `fallback` is provided, the `value` argument to `set` is optional
and `get_maybe` is omitted from the type.

overloads.typeSignature: <T>(): { get: (error_message?: string | undefined) => T; get_maybe: () => T | undefined; set: (value: T) => T; }
overloads.returnType: { get: (error_message?: string | undefined) => T; get_maybe: () => T | undefined; set: (value: T) => T; }

declarations.returnType: { get: () => T; set: (value?: T | undefined) => T; }

declarations.name: ContextmenuRoot
declarations.docComment:
Touch event handler for tap-then-longpress bypass detection.

This allows users to access the native context menu by performing a tap
followed by a longpress/rightclick within a specified time window.
The bypass gesture is useful for accessing browser features like text selection
or the native context menu when the Fuz contextmenu would normally override it.

Note: preventDefault is not called as we're only observing touch patterns,
not intercepting them. The actual bypass happens in on_window_contextmenu.

props.name: open_offset_x
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer X position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: open_offset_y
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer Y position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: bypass_with_tap_then_longpress
props.description:
Whether to detect tap-then-longpress to bypass the Fuz contextmenu.
This allows access to the system contextmenu by tapping once then rightclicking/long-pressing.
Setting to `false` disables the gesture.

props.name: bypass_window
props.description:
The number of milliseconds between taps to detect a gesture that bypasses the Fuz contextmenu.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.
If the duration is too long, it'll detect more false positives and interrupt normal usage,
but too short and some people will have difficulty performing the gesture.

props.name: bypass_move_tolerance
props.description:
The number of pixels the pointer can be moved between taps to detect a tap-then-longpress.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.

props.name: scoped
props.description: If `true`, wraps `children` with a div and listens to events on it instead of the window.

props.name: link_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering link entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic link detection.
Defaults to `link_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuLinkEntry`.

props.name: text_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering copy text entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic copy text detection.
Defaults to `text_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuTextEntry`.

props.name: separator_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering separator entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic separator rendering.
Defaults to `separator_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuSeparator`.

declarations.name: ContextmenuRootForSafariCompatibility
declarations.docComment:
Blocks the next click event. Set to true when a longpress completes to prevent
iOS's synthesized click from activating the first menu item.

props.name: longpress_move_tolerance
props.description: The number of pixels the pointer can be moved without canceling `longpress`.

props.name: longpress_duration
props.description: The number of milliseconds after a touch starts before opening the Fuz contextmenu.

props.name: bypass_with_tap_then_longpress
props.description:
Whether to detect tap-then-longpress to bypass the Fuz contextmenu.
This allows access to the system contextmenu by tapping once then long-pressing.
Setting to `false` disables the gesture.

props.name: bypass_window
props.description:
The number of milliseconds between taps to detect a gesture that bypasses the Fuz contextmenu.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.
If the duration is too long, it'll detect more false positives and interrupt normal usage,
but too short and some people will have difficulty performing the gesture.

props.name: bypass_move_tolerance
props.description:
The number of pixels the pointer can be moved between taps to detect a tap-then-longpress.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.

props.name: open_offset_x
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer X position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: open_offset_y
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer Y position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: scoped
props.description: If `true`, wraps `children` with a div and listens to events on it instead of the window.

props.name: link_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering link entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic link detection.
Defaults to `link_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuLinkEntry`.

props.name: text_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering copy text entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic copy text detection.
Defaults to `text_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuTextEntry`.

props.name: separator_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering separator entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic separator rendering.
Defaults to `separator_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuSeparator`.

props.name: oncopy
props.type: ClipboardEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement> & ((text: string | null, e: MouseEvent) => void)

props.name: icon_button
props.description: Defaults to `true`, ignored if `children` is provided.

declarations.name: csp_directives_of_fuzdev
declarations.docComment:
Per-directive sources that allow full interop between fuzdev/sister sites
(`*.fuz.dev`, `*.zzz.software`) — loading assets and APIs from each other,
and iframing in both directions.

Pass into `create_csp_directives({extend: [csp_directives_of_fuzdev]})`.
Intended for sites within the ecosystem; external apps usually want a
narrower, hand-written allow-list.

Deliberately scoped — `script-src`, `style-src`, `worker-src`, etc. are not
granted, even between sister sites. Add at the call site if a specific page
needs them.

declarations.name: CspDirectiveSourcesMap
declarations.docComment:
Per-directive map of source arrays — accepted as `extend` layer entries.
Excludes directives like `'upgrade-insecure-requests'` (boolean) that can't be appended to.

declarations.name: CreateCspDirectivesOptions
declarations.docComment:
Options for `create_csp_directives`.

The pipeline runs in three stages:
1. `replace_defaults` sets the starting state (defaults to `csp_directive_value_defaults`).
2. `extend` appends sources per directive, layered left to right.
3. `overrides` replaces or removes per-directive values as a final pass.

members.name: replace_defaults
members.docComment:
Starting values per directive — *wholesale replaces* the library defaults.

- Omitted: uses `csp_directive_value_defaults` (the curated library defaults).
- Provided: exactly the directives you list, nothing else inherited.
  Anything not listed is **absent** from the starting state — including security defaults
  like `default-src: 'none'`. To tweak a single directive while keeping the rest, use
  `extend` (to append) or `overrides` (to replace per-key) instead.
- `{}`: starts blank with no directives.

`null` is not accepted (top-level or per-key) — omit the option to use library defaults,
pass `{}` to start blank, or use `overrides` to remove a specific directive.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op).

members.name: extend
members.docComment:
Sources to append per directive, layered left to right.
Each entry is a partial map; values append to the result of `replace_defaults` and prior entries.
Values are deduplicated within and across layers.

Only array-typed directives can be extended (boolean directives like `upgrade-insecure-requests`
are excluded by the type). Throws if any entry attempts to extend a directive whose current
value is `['none']` — use `replace_defaults` or `overrides` to opt into default-deny directives.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op) — supports conditional patterns like
`{'connect-src': is_prod ? [API_URL] : undefined}`. Per-key `null` throws — `extend` only
appends; use `overrides: { 'X': null }` to remove a directive.

members.name: overrides
members.docComment:
Final-pass per-directive overrides. Replaces the directive value or removes it entirely.
Pass `null` to remove a directive from the output.

Highest precedence — wins over `replace_defaults` and `extend`.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op) — distinct from `null`, which removes.

declarations.name: create_csp_directives
declarations.docComment:
Builds a CSP directives map for use with SvelteKit's `kit.csp.directives` option.

Restrictive by default; opt into specific permissions via `extend` (append) or
`overrides` (replace). Designed to read as an audit log: every user-added source
is named at exactly one site in the source code. Library defaults are inherited
unless you opt out via `replace_defaults`.

Validation:
- Unknown directive keys throw.
- Extending a `['none']` directive throws (use `replace_defaults`/`overrides` to opt in).
- `null` for `replace_defaults` (top-level or per-key) throws — omit the option for library
  defaults, pass `{}` to start blank, or use `overrides` to remove a specific directive.
- `null` per-key in `extend` throws (use `overrides` for removal).
- `undefined` per-key is treated as omitted in all three stages.
- Non-object entries in `extend` (`null`, `undefined`, primitives) throw with a friendly error.
- Output is validated to ensure `'none'` never appears alongside other tokens,
  that no directive ends up with an empty array (use `['none']` to forbid all),
  and that every source array contains only strings.

Things like rendering to a string are out of scope and left to SvelteKit.

declarations.name: csp_directive_value_defaults
declarations.docComment:
The library CSP directive defaults — directives enabled out of the box.
Prioritizes safety but loosens around media and styles, relying on defense-in-depth.
WASM compile is allowed (`'wasm-unsafe-eval'` on `script-src` and `worker-src`); `eval` is not.

Directives not listed here (`report-to`, `require-trusted-types-for`, `trusted-types`,
`sandbox`) are intentionally absent by default — opt in via `replace_defaults` or `overrides`.

Customizable via `CreateCspDirectivesOptions.replace_defaults`.
declarations.typeSignature: Partial<{ "default-src": (CspSource | CspActionSource)[]; "script-src": (CspSource | CspActionSource)[]; "script-src-elem": CspSources; ... 20 more ...; sandbox: ("allow-downloads-without-user-activation" | ... 11 more ... | "allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation")[]; }>

declarations.name: csp_directive_specs
declarations.docComment:
Static data descriptors for the CSP directives.
Fuz excludes deprecated directives, so those are intentionally omitted,
but any newer missing directives are bugs.

declarations.name: Declaration

Rich runtime representation of an exported declaration.

Wraps svelte-docinfo's `DeclarationJson` discriminated union (on `kind`)
with Svelte 5 reactive derivations and computed URLs.
Kind-specific fields are accessed via the `field()` helper since
not all fields exist on all variants.

declarations.name: DeclarationDetail
declarations.docComment:
Renders the full detail view for an exported declaration.
Handles all svelte-docinfo declaration kinds (function, class, interface,
type, variable, enum, component, snippet) and their kind-specific fields
including parameters, props, members, overloads, intersects, and more.

props.name: hash
props.description: URL fragment to append, with or without the `#`.

props.name: full
props.description:
Link to the library's own deployed docs (`url_api_full`) instead of a
site-local path (`url_api`). Use when rendering a foreign library on a
different site, e.g. an aggregator, so links don't dangle locally.

props.name: eager
props.description:
Children are lazily rendered by default,
enabling transitions and improving performance, sometimes significantly.
Pass `true` to render children eagerly to gain
`ctrl+f` functionality while trading away the benefits.
Consider a `<details>` element instead of making this component eager.

modules.moduleComment: Like `details` but renders children lazily by default.

props.name: index
props.description: index 0 is under 1 is under 2 etc -- the topmost dialog is the last in the array

props.name: content_selector
props.description:
If provided, prevents clicks that would close the dialog
from bubbling past any elements matching this selector.

declarations.name: to_dialog_params
declarations.docComment:
This helper function is needed to construct `DialogParams` with type safety.
It uses TypeScript's inferred generics for functions,
which do not work for plain objects as of v5.0.4.
* `ContextmenuParams` uses a similar strategy.
declarations.typeSignature: <T extends Component<any>>(Component: T, props: ComponentProps<T>, dialog_props?: any): DialogParams<T>

declarations.name: DialogParams
declarations.docComment:
This pattern is based on:
https://github.com/ivanhofer/sveltekit-typescript-showcase/blob/main/src/01-props/09-svelte-component/Component.svelte
The main limitation is that the generic cannot be inferred automatically,
so we use `to_dialog_params` to construct instances in most cases.
Definining `DialogParams` with no concrete `T` lacks typechecking for `props`.

Unlike `slugify` from `@fuzdev/fuz_util/path.js` which lowercases,

and `async_status` produce distinct fragment IDs.
Used by the `Tome` documentation system for heading and section anchors.

Called when the attachment's observer is disconnected,
either by the user calling disconnect or the attachment being destroyed.

A value of `1` disconnects after `el` enters and leaves the viewport one time,
similar to 'once' for an event.
`0` disables and `undefined` or a negative number like `-1` never disconnects.

Uses the lazy function pattern to optimize reactivity:
callbacks can update without recreating the observer, preserving state.

For generic package/repository URL helpers, see `@fuzdev/fuz_util/package_helpers.js`.
