members.name: publishing_order
members.docComment: Topological publishing order, or `null` when prod/peer cycles prevent ordering.

declarations.name: analyze_repos
declarations.docComment:
Builds the dependency graph and runs cycle/wildcard analysis, tolerating cycles
(reports rather than throws). The shared core of `gitops_analyze` and
`gitops_validate`, which format the result themselves — so this stays silent
(no `log`) to avoid emitting a redundant "Analyzing dependencies" line.

modules.moduleComment:
Shared dependency graph validation logic used across multiple workflows.

Consolidates graph building, cycle detection, and publishing order computation
that was duplicated in three places: `multi_repo_publisher.ts`, `publishing_plan.ts`,
and `gitops_analyze.task.ts`.

Options pattern supports different behaviors: analyze can tolerate cycles for
reporting, while publish must throw on production cycles.

See also: `dependency_graph.ts` for core graph data structure and algorithms.

declarations.name: LocalRepo
declarations.docComment:
Fully loaded local repo with `Library` and extracted dependency data.
Does not extend `LocalRepoPath` - `Library` is source of truth for name/repo_url/etc.

members.name: package_json
members.docComment: The repo's full `package.json` (with `dependencies`/`devDependencies`).

declarations.name: LocalRepoPath
declarations.docComment:
A repo that has been located on the filesystem (path exists).
Used before loading - just filesystem/git concerns.

declarations.name: LocalRepoMissing
declarations.docComment: A repo that is missing from the filesystem (needs cloning).

declarations.name: local_repo_load
declarations.docComment:
Loads repo data, optionally syncing the working tree first.

When `sync` is `false` (the default for read-only diagnostics like
`gitops_analyze`/`gitops_plan`), the repo is loaded exactly as it sits on
disk — no branch switch, pull, install, or clean-workspace check. This makes
those commands safe to run on an active workspace with uncommitted changes or
feature branches checked out.

When `sync` is `true` (used by `gitops_sync`), the working tree is brought in
line with the configured branch first:
1. Records current commit hash (for detecting changes)
2. Switches to target branch if needed (requires clean workspace unless `allow_dirty`)
3. Pulls latest changes from remote (skipped for local-only repos)
4. Validates workspace is clean after pull (skipped if `allow_dirty`)
5. Auto-installs dependencies if `package.json` changed

Either way it then:
6. Loads `library_json` via `library_load_from_repo` (svelte-docinfo analysis)
7. Creates `Library` and extracts dependency maps
declarations.typeSignature: ({ local_repo_path, log: _log, git_ops, npm_ops, sync, allow_dirty, }: { local_repo_path: LocalRepoPath; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; sync?: boolean | undefined; allow_dirty?: boolean | undefined; }): Promise<...>

throws.description: syncing fails (dirty workspace, branch switch, install) or analysis fails

parameters.type: { local_repo_path: LocalRepoPath; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; sync?: boolean | undefined; allow_dirty?: boolean | undefined; }

parameters.type: { resolved_config: ResolvedGitopsConfig; repos_dir: string; gitops_config: GitopsConfig; download: boolean; log?: Logger | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; }

members.name: events
members.docComment: Structured event sink; defaults to capture-only (events surface on the result).

members.name: events
members.docComment: The structured event stream for this run, in emission order.

members.name: summary
members.docComment: Tallied outcome, derived from `events`.

members.name: plan_errors
members.docComment: Plan errors that blocked (wetrun) or would block (dry run) publishing; empty when clean.

members.name: plan_warnings
members.docComment: Non-blocking plan warnings (e.g. the fixed-point iteration limit).

declarations.name: execute_publishing_plan
declarations.docComment:
Executes a frozen publishing plan in a single linear pass — the "dumb executor" half of
the zap model. The plan is the single source of truth; this re-derives nothing.

Fails loud when the plan couldn't be fully computed (`plan.errors`): a wetrun aborts
before any side effect, a dry run still reports the partial cascade but returns
`ok: false`. This is the single error gate — `--no-plan` can't bypass it.
declarations.typeSignature: (repos: LocalRepo[], plan: PublishingPlan, options: PublishingOptions): Promise<PublishingResult>

declarations.name: group_dependency_updates
declarations.docComment:
Groups dependency updates by dependent package — `dependent → (dependency → new
version)`, the shape `update_package_json` consumes. Restricted by `predicate` (e.g.
prod/peer for a given package, or all dev deps) and to dependencies that actually
published this run, so a failed/aborted publish never propagates to its dependents.
declarations.typeSignature: (updates: DependencyUpdate[], published: Map<string, PublishedVersion>, predicate: (update: DependencyUpdate) => boolean): Map<...>

declarations.name: check_package_available
declarations.typeSignature: (pkg: string, version: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<boolean>

declarations.name: wait_for_package
declarations.docComment:
Waits for package version to propagate to NPM registry.

Uses exponential backoff with jitter to avoid hammering registry.
Logs progress every 5 attempts. Respects timeout to avoid infinite waits.

Critical for multi-repo publishing: ensures published packages are available
before updating dependent packages.
declarations.typeSignature: (pkg: string, version: string, options?: WaitOptions): Promise<void>

throws.description: timeout reached or max attempts exceeded

declarations.name: get_package_info
declarations.docComment:
Fetches package metadata from NPM registry.

Returns name and latest version. Returns null if package doesn't exist
or registry is unreachable.
declarations.typeSignature: (pkg: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<PackageInfo | null>
declarations.returnDescription: package info or null on error/not found

declarations.name: package_exists
declarations.typeSignature: (pkg: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<boolean>

declarations.name: default_gitops_operations
declarations.docComment: Combined default operations for all gitops functionality.

modules.moduleComment:
Production implementations of operations interfaces.

Provides real git, npm, fs, and build operations for production use.
For interface definitions and dependency injection pattern, see `operations.ts`.

declarations.name: ChangesetOperations
declarations.docComment: Changeset operations for reading and predicting versions from `.changeset/*.md` files.

members.name: has_changesets
members.docComment:
Checks if a repo has any changeset files.
Returns true if changesets exist, false if none found.

members.name: read_changesets
members.docComment:
Reads all changeset files from a repo.
Returns array of changeset info, or error if reading fails.

members.name: predict_next_version
members.docComment:
Predicts the next version based on changesets.
Returns null if no changesets found (expected, not an error).
Returns error `Result` if changesets exist but can't be read/parsed.

declarations.name: GitOperations
declarations.docComment:
Git operations for branch management, commits, tags, and workspace state.
All operations return `Result` instead of throwing errors.

members.name: current_branch_name
members.docComment: Gets the current branch name.

members.name: current_commit_hash
members.docComment: Gets the current commit hash.

members.name: check_clean_workspace

members.name: pull

members.name: switch_branch

members.name: has_changes
members.docComment:
Checks whether the working tree has any changes — staged, unstaged, or
untracked (`git status --porcelain`). Broader than `list_uncommitted_files`,
which reports only tracked working-tree changes relative to HEAD.

members.name: list_uncommitted_files
members.docComment:
Lists uncommitted files in the working tree (`git diff --name-only HEAD`),
i.e. working-tree changes relative to HEAD (not a diff between two refs).

declarations.name: BuildOperations

NPM registry operations for package availability checks and authentication.
Includes exponential backoff for waiting on package propagation.

Waits for a package version to be available on NPM.
Uses exponential backoff with configurable timeout.

declarations.docComment:
Preflight validation operations to ensure repos are ready for publishing.
Validates workspace state, branches, builds, and npm authentication.

members.name: run_preflight_checks
members.docComment: Runs preflight validation checks before publishing.

declarations.name: FsOperations
declarations.docComment:
File system operations for reading and writing files.

Errors are typed via `FsError` (`not_found | permission_denied |
already_exists | io_error`) so callers can branch on `kind` instead of
regex-matching `message`. See `@fuzdev/fuz_util/fs.js`.

members.name: readFile
members.docComment: Reads a file from the file system.

members.name: writeFile

members.name: exists
members.docComment: Checks if a path exists on the file system.

declarations.name: GitopsOperations
declarations.docComment:
Combined operations interface grouping all gitops functionality.
This is the main interface injected into publishing and validation workflows.

members.name: stdout
members.docComment: This function should call log methods directly for colored/styled output.

declarations.name: format_and_output
declarations.docComment:
Formats data and outputs to file or stdout based on options.

Supports three formats:
- stdout: Uses logger for colored/styled output (cannot use with `--outfile`)
- json: Stringified JSON
- markdown: Formatted markdown text
declarations.typeSignature: <T>(data: T, formatters: OutputFormatters<T>, options: OutputOptions): Promise<void>

throws.description: stdout format used with `outfile`, or if logger missing for stdout

props.name: repo
props.type: { pkg_json: PkgJson; } | { url: string; pkg_json: null; }

declarations.name: DEFAULT_REPOS_DIR
declarations.docComment:
Default repos directory relative to gitops config file.
Resolves to the parent of the directory with the config
(e.g., `~/dev/repo/gitops.config.ts` resolves to `~/dev/`).

declarations.name: run_preflight_checks
declarations.docComment:
Validates all requirements before publishing can proceed.

Performs comprehensive pre-flight validation:
- Clean workspaces (100% clean required - no uncommitted changes)
- Correct branch (usually main)
- Changesets present (unless `skip_changesets`=true)
- Builds successful (fail-fast to prevent broken state)
- Git remote reachability
- NPM authentication with username
- NPM registry connectivity

Build validation runs BEFORE any publishing to prevent the scenario where
version is bumped but build fails, leaving repo in broken state.
declarations.typeSignature: ({ repos, preflight_options, git_ops, npm_ops, build_ops, changeset_ops, }: RunPreflightChecksOptions): Promise<PreflightResult>
declarations.returnDescription: result with `ok`=false if any errors, plus warnings and detailed status

declarations.name: PublishGate
declarations.docComment: What the task should do with a generated plan before executing the cascade.

members.name: wetrun
members.docComment: A real publish (`--wetrun`); a dry run never prompts.

members.name: show_plan
members.docComment: Show the plan and confirm (`plan`); `--no-plan` skips the prompt.

declarations.name: decide_publish_gate
declarations.docComment:
Decides whether a publish run must block, prompt for confirmation, or proceed without
prompting, from the pre-execution inputs.

- `blocked`: a real publish whose plan has errors — fail loud before prompting. The executor
  enforces this too, so `--no-plan` can't bypass the gate; this branch only avoids prompting
  for (and printing the "this will publish" banner of) a plan that can't run.
- `confirm`: a real publish that shows its plan — the user must confirm interactively.
- `proceed`: a dry run, or a `--no-plan` real publish — no prompt.

declarations.name: publish_run_failed
declarations.docComment:
Whether a finished run should exit non-zero: an unsuccessful result, or a fatal error thrown
out of the executor.

modules.moduleComment:
Pure decision logic for the `gitops_publish` task's interactive gating.

Extracted from `gitops_publish.task.ts` so the gating table is unit-testable without driving
readline or `process.exit`. The task keeps the IO — prompt, exit, logging — at its edge and
consults these for every branch.

declarations.name: PublishStepVia
declarations.docComment: How a package's version bump arises in the plan.

declarations.name: PublishStep
declarations.docComment: One ordered side-effect a wetrun would perform.

members.name: deploy
members.docComment: Include the deploy phase (the publisher only deploys with `--deploy`).

declarations.name: derive_publish_steps
declarations.docComment:
Derives the ordered side-effects a wetrun would perform from a frozen plan.

Reads only `publishing_order`, `version_changes`, and `dependency_updates` — the same data
`execute_publishing_plan` consumes, in the same order — so the preview reflects the real
pass. A dependency is only propagated to its dependents if it actually publishes this run.

declarations.name: format_publish_steps
declarations.docComment:
Formats steps as human-readable lines (one per step) for stdout and markdown output.
Returns a single placeholder line when there are no side effects.

modules.moduleComment:
Side-effect preview for a publishing plan.

`derive_publish_steps` linearizes a frozen `PublishingPlan` into the ordered side-effects
a `--wetrun` would perform, mirroring `execute_publishing_plan`'s pass so the preview and
the executor read the same plan data and can't drift. Pure — no side effects.

declarations.name: PublishingEventHandler
declarations.docComment: A sink for publishing events.

declarations.name: CapturingEventHandler
declarations.docComment: A `capture_handler` also exposes the events it has collected.

declarations.name: null_handler
declarations.docComment: Drops every event. The default when no handler is supplied.

declarations.name: capture_handler
declarations.docComment: Collects events in memory. Used to build the run report and in tests.

declarations.name: stdout_handler
declarations.docComment:
Writes each event as one JSON object per line (JSON-lines) to `process.stdout`.
Write failures are swallowed — the stream is observability, not control flow.

declarations.name: multi_handler
declarations.docComment: Fans an event out to every handler in order.

declarations.name: masking_handler
declarations.docComment: Wraps a handler, masking secrets in each event's string fields before forwarding.
declarations.typeSignature: (inner: PublishingEventHandler, mask?: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { ...; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; }) => { ...; } | ... 8 more ... | { ...; }): PublishingEventHandler

parameters.name: inner
parameters.description: the handler to forward masked events to

parameters.name: mask
parameters.type: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | .....
parameters.description: the masking function, defaults to `mask_secrets`

declarations.name: redact_secrets
declarations.docComment: Redacts known secret shapes from a string.

declarations.name: mask_secrets
declarations.docComment: Returns a copy of the event with secrets redacted from its string-valued fields.
declarations.typeSignature: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; }): { ...; } | ... 8 more ... | { ...; }

parameters.name: event
parameters.type: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more...

declarations.returnType: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more...

modules.moduleComment:
Composable sinks for the publishing event stream.

A `PublishingEventHandler` is anything that can receive a `PublishingEvent`. Handlers
compose: `multi_handler` fans out, `masking_handler` redacts secrets then forwards.
Emission is best-effort and synchronous — an observability sink must never fail or
slow a run. The default sink is `null_handler` (drops everything).

declarations.name: PublishingErrorCode
declarations.docComment:
Coarse triage classification for a failed package. Lets consumers branch on
failure kind without parsing the message.
declarations.typeSignature: ZodEnum<{ publish: "publish"; network: "network"; auth: "auth"; dependency: "dependency"; build: "build"; drift: "drift"; other: "other"; }>

declarations.name: PublishingRunSummary
declarations.docComment: Tallied outcome of a publishing run, derived from its events via `summarize_events`.
declarations.typeSignature: ZodObject<{ total: ZodNumber; published: ZodNumber; failed: ZodNumber; skipped: ZodNumber; duration: ZodNumber; }, $strict>

declarations.name: PublishingEvent
declarations.docComment:
A single structured event emitted during a publishing run. Tagged on `event` so the
union serializes as one self-describing JSON object per event (JSON-lines on the wire).
declarations.typeSignature: ZodDiscriminatedUnion<[ZodObject<{ event: ZodLiteral<"run_started">; wetrun: ZodBoolean; total: ZodNumber; }, $strict>, ZodObject<{ event: ZodLiteral<"package_skipped">; name: ZodString; reason: ZodString; }, $strict>, ... 7 more ..., ZodObject<...>], "event">

declarations.name: summarize_events
declarations.docComment:
Derives a run summary from the captured event list — the single canonical path from
events to summary, so the `run_finished` summary always agrees with the stream.
Call before emitting `run_finished` (which is not itself counted).
declarations.typeSignature: (events: ({ event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; })[], duration: number): { ...; }

parameters.name: events
parameters.type: ({ event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 mor...
parameters.description: the events captured so far this run

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: wall-clock duration in milliseconds

declarations.returnType: { total: number; published: number; failed: number; skipped: number; duration: number; }

modules.moduleComment:
Structured events for multi-repo publishing.

Publishing emits a stream of tagged events alongside its human-readable logging,
so machine consumers (CI, dashboards) can follow a run step by step. Every run
opens with a `run_started` event carrying `wetrun`: when `false`, the run is a dry
run and every `package_completed` is a prediction (its `commit` is `'simulated'`)
rather than an applied change. A run's `run_finished` summary is derived from the
same event list via `summarize_events`, so the stream and the summary never drift.

Events are consumed through a `PublishingEventHandler` sink (see
`publishing_event_handler.ts`).

declarations.name: calculate_dependency_updates
declarations.docComment:
Calculates all dependency updates between packages based on predicted versions.

Iterates through all repos, checking prod, peer, and dev dependencies to find
which packages will need dependency version bumps after publishing.

Also tracks "breaking cascades" - when a breaking change propagates to dependents.
declarations.typeSignature: (repos: LocalRepo[], predicted_versions: Map<string, string>, breaking_packages: Set<string>): { dependency_updates: DependencyUpdate[]; breaking_cascades: Map<...>; }

declarations.name: get_required_bump_for_dependencies
declarations.docComment:
Determines the required bump type for a package based on its dependency updates.

Returns null if no prod/peer dependency updates, otherwise returns the minimum
required bump type (major for breaking deps, patch otherwise).

Respects pre-1.0 semver conventions (minor for breaking in 0.x).

Generates a publishing plan showing what would happen during publishing.
Shows version changes, dependency updates, and breaking change cascades.
Uses fixed-point iteration to resolve transitive cascades.

modules.moduleComment:
Generic repository operations for scripts that work across repos.

Provides lightweight utilities for:
- Getting repo paths from gitops config (without full git sync)
- Walking files in repos with sensible exclusions
- Common exclusion patterns for node/svelte projects

For full git sync/clone functionality, use `get_gitops_ready()` from `gitops_task_helpers`.

declarations.name: RepoJson
declarations.docComment:
Serialized repo data as stored in `repos.ts` (JSON).

`package_json` is the repo's full `package.json`, carried alongside
`library_json` because gitops reads `dependencies`/`devDependencies` for the
publishing cascade — fields the curated `LibraryJson.pkg_json` omits.

declarations.name: Repo
declarations.docComment:
Runtime repo with `Library` composition for package metadata.

Wraps a `Library` instance and adds GitHub-specific data (CI status, PRs).
Convenience getters delegate to `this.library.*` for common properties.

members.docComment: The repo's full `package.json` (with `dependencies`/`devDependencies`).

members.name: pkg_json
members.docComment: Curated package identity, delegating to `library`. Distinct from the full `package_json`.

declarations.name: BumpType
declarations.docComment: A semver bump kind: `major`, `minor`, or `patch`.

declarations.name: strip_version_prefix
declarations.docComment: Strips version prefix (^, ~, >=, <=, etc) from a version string.

declarations.name: get_version_prefix
declarations.docComment: Gets the version prefix (^, ~, >=, <=, or empty string).

declarations.name: normalize_version_for_comparison
declarations.docComment:
Normalizes version string for comparison.

Strips prefixes (^, ~, >=) to get bare version number.
Handles wildcards as-is. Used by `needs_update` to compare versions.
examples:
```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('^1.2.3') // '1.2.3'
```

```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('>=2.0.0') // '2.0.0'
```

```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('*') // '*'
```

declarations.name: get_update_prefix
declarations.docComment:
Determines version prefix to use when updating dependencies.

Strategy:
- Wildcard (*): Use caret (^) as default
- Has existing prefix: Preserve it (^, ~, >=, <=, etc)
- No prefix: Use default_strategy

This preserves user intent while handling wildcard replacements sensibly.

parameters.name: default_strategy
parameters.description: prefix to use when no existing prefix found

declarations.name: is_breaking_change
declarations.docComment:
Determines if a bump is a breaking change based on semver rules.
Pre-1.0: minor bumps are breaking
1.0+: major bumps are breaking

declarations.name: required_bump_for_dependency_update
declarations.docComment:
The bump a package must take when one of its prod/peer dependencies updates.
Pre-1.0: `minor` for a breaking dependency, otherwise `patch`.
1.0+: `major` for a breaking dependency, otherwise `patch`.

Single source of truth for the dependency-driven bump rule, shared by the plan
(`get_required_bump_for_dependencies`) and the auto-changeset generator
(`calculate_required_bump`) so the two never drift.

parameters.name: current_version
parameters.description: the package's current version, used to detect the pre-1.0 regime

parameters.name: has_breaking_deps
parameters.description: whether any updated dependency is a breaking change

declarations.name: compare_bump_types
declarations.docComment: Compares bump types. Returns positive if a > b, negative if a < b, 0 if equal.

modules.moduleComment:
Hand-written types for the public `@fuzdev` stack dependency graph.

The generated `stack_graph.ts` (under `src/routes/docs/stack/`) re-exports
these and provides the baked `stack_nodes` / `stack_edges` data. The pure
layout in `stack_layout.ts` produces the positioned `Stack_Node`s.

declarations.name: ROW_GAP
declarations.docComment: Vertical spacing between adjacent layers.

declarations.name: COL_GAP
declarations.docComment: Horizontal spacing between adjacent nodes within a layer.

declarations.name: Stack_Node_Input
declarations.docComment: Raw node input to `compute_stack_layout` — the positioned fields are filled in by the layout.

members.name: name
members.docComment: Short repo/display name, e.g. `fuz_util`, `fuz.dev`.

members.name: description
members.docComment: From the manifest `description` field; empty string if none.

declarations.name: compute_stack_layout
declarations.docComment:
Computes baked layout positions for the stack graph.

The full `edges` list is used only to derive the layering; the emitted edge
list is owned by the caller (the generator emits every edge, including the
`dev` ones this layout cuts).
declarations.typeSignature: (nodes: readonly Stack_Node_Input[], edges: readonly Stack_Edge[]): Stack_Node[]

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description: raw nodes keyed by `name`

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: every dependency edge (`prod`, `peer`, and `dev`)

declarations.returnDescription: positioned nodes sorted by `name`, with `layer`, `fan_in`, `x`, `y` filled in

modules.moduleComment:
Pure, deterministic layout for the public `@fuzdev` stack dependency graph.

`compute_stack_layout` takes raw nodes (name/category/language/description)
plus the full edge list and bakes in `layer`, `fan_in`, and world-space
`x`/`y` coordinates. No randomness — running it twice on the same input
yields byte-identical output, which keeps the generated `stack_graph.ts`
stable across `gro gen` runs.

of `BackendActionsApi`. Async methods (request_response, remote_notification,
async local_call) return `Promise<Result<...>>` and accept an optional
`RpcClientCallOptions` second arg that threads `signal`, `transport_name`,
and `queue` through to the peer. Sync local_call methods return values
directly.

declarations.name: all_action_specs
declarations.docComment:
Canonical action spec list — fuz_app's protocol actions (`heartbeat`,
`cancel`) plus zzz-owned specs. Single source of truth for codegen and
runtime registries (`Frontend.action_registry`, `Backend.action_registry`).

Codegen helpers default-filter protocol actions out of typed surfaces
(`FrontendActionsApi`, `BackendActionHandlers`, `ActionMethod`, etc.) since
they're dispatcher-owned. Server WS registration pairs protocol specs with
fuz_app-shipped handlers via `protocol_actions`; iteration over
`all_action_specs` skips them via `is_protocol_action_method` to avoid
double-registration. Adding a future protocol action requires no consumer
edit — fuz_app's `protocol_action_specs` is the upstream canonical bundle.

declarations.name: Action
declarations.docComment: Represents a single action in the system, tracking its full lifecycle through action events.

members.name: pending
members.docComment:
Returns true if the action is still pending (not in a terminal state).
An action is complete when it reaches a terminal phase (where next phase is null)
AND the step is terminal (handled or failed).

members.name: success
members.docComment:
Returns true if the action completed successfully.
Success means: action is complete (terminal phase + step), step is 'handled', and no error.

Any options besides these declared ones are ignored,

declarations.name: get_global_cell_count
declarations.docComment:
A monotonic id for cell instances on the client.
This is not reactive and never changes on the instance,
whereas `id` can change. It's useful for ordering -
the motivating usecase was correctly sorting objects with the same `created` millisecond.

This may cause issues based on how data gets queried, but at least it's stable once loaded.
However we may need to revisit this if it causes problems.

declarations.name: Cell
declarations.docComment:
The `Cell` is the base class for schema-driven data models in Zzz.
The goals are still evolving, but a main idea is to have fully reactive state
that can be flexibly snapshotted and reinstantiated in a typesafe, extensible system
that uses normal Svelte patterns.

The design aims for:

- Integration with Svelte's reactivity, encouraging single-depth inheritance
		with Svelte class patterns for both persistent and ephemeral state
- Schema-driven parsing/validation and JSON serialization/deserialization
		(supporting snapshot and restore/replay patterns) via Zod
- Custom property encoding/decoding for complex types,
		and no boilerplate for schema-inferrable properties
- Lifecycle management with generic instantiation/registration and disposal
		(conceptually a WIP, partially implemented)
- Runtime type metadata for reflection

Cells are automatically registered in the global registry by `id`,
making them discoverable and referenceable throughout the system.
Each cell has common properties including `id` and `created`/`updated` timestamps.

There are currently a lot of rough edges and missing features!
My hope is that this could be generic enough to extract to a library, but it's not there yet.
I assume there's a really nice design in this space that takes full advantage of Svelte runes.

Many things will be possible with this pattern, but it's still a work in progress.

members.name: decoders
members.docComment:
Type-safe decoders for custom field decoding.
Override in subclasses to handle special field types.

Each decoder function takes a value of any type and should either:
1. Return a value (including null) to be assigned to the property
2. Return undefined to use the default decoding behavior
3. Return `HANDLED` to indicate the decoder has fully processed the property
   (virtual properties MUST return `HANDLED` if they exist in schema but not in class)

members.name: constructor
members.typeSignature: <TSchema extends z.ZodType = ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>(schema: TSchema, options: CellOptions<TSchema>): Cell<TSchema>

members.name: init
members.docComment:
Initialize the instance with `options.json` data if provided.
Must be called before using the instance -
the current pattern is calling it at the end of subclass constructors.

We should investigate deferring to callers so e.g. instantiating from the registry
would init automatically, subclasses need no init logic (which can get unwieldy with inheritance),
and then callers could always do custom init patterns if they wanted.

Can't be called automatically by the `Cell`'s constructor because
the subclass' constructor needs to run first to support field initialization.
A design goal behind the `Cell` is to support normal TS and Svelte patterns
with the most power and least intrusion. (there's a balance to find from Zzz's POV)

members.name: dispose
members.docComment: Clean up resources when this cell is no longer needed.

members.name: [Symbol.dispose]
members.docComment:
This is not supported in Safari, don't rely on this yet.
Uncomment temporarily to experiment in dev.

members.name: register
members.docComment:
Register this cell in the global cell registry.
Called automatically by `init()`.

members.name: unregister
members.docComment:
Unregister this cell from the global cell registry.
Called automatically by `dispose()`.

members.name: toJSON
members.docComment: For Svelte's $snapshot.

members.name: to_json
members.docComment:
Encodes the cell's serializable state as JSON.
Use the derived `cell.json` if you don't need a fresh copy.

members.name: set_json
members.docComment:
Apply JSON data to this instance.
Overwrites all properties including 'id'.
Special-cases `created` and `updated` for synchronization.

members.name: set_json_partial
members.docComment:
Update only the specified properties on this instance.
Preserves current values for any properties not included in the input.

members.name: encode_property
members.docComment:
Encode a value during serialization. Can be overridden for custom encoding logic.
Defaults to Svelte's `$state.snapshot`,
which handles most cases and uses `toJSON` when available,
so overriding `to_json` is sufficient for most cases before overriding `encode_property`.

members.name: decode_property
members.docComment:
Decode a value based on its schema type information.
This handles instantiating classes, transforming arrays, and special types.

Complex property types might require custom handling in parser functions
rather than using this general decoding mechanism.
members.typeSignature: <K extends SchemaKeys<TSchema>>(value: unknown, key: K): any

members.name: assign_property
members.docComment:
Process a single schema property during JSON deserialization.
This method handles the workflow for mapping schema properties to instance properties.

Flow:
1. If a decoder exists for this property, try to use it
2. If decoder returns `HANDLED`, consider the property fully handled (short circuit)
3. If decoder returns a value other than `HANDLED` or undefined, use that value
4. If decoder returns undefined, fall through to standard decoding
5. For properties not directly represented in the class instance but defined
   in the schema, the decoder MUST return `HANDLED` to indicate proper handling

members.docComment: Generic clone method that works for any subclass.
members.typeSignature: (json?: input<TSchema> | undefined, options?: CellOptions<TSchema> | undefined): this

members.name: zzz_dir
members.docComment:
The `zzz_dir` is the path to Zzz's primary directory on the server's filesystem.
The server's `ScopedFs` instance restricts operations to this directory.
The value is `undefined` when uninitialized,
`null` when loading, and `''` when disabled or no server.

members.name: scoped_dirs
members.docComment: Additional filesystem paths the server can access for user files.

declarations.name: estimate_token_count
declarations.docComment:
Quick and dirty token count estimate using `ESTIMATED_CHARS_PER_TOKEN`.
Real tokenizers are heavy and of little benefit for our cases right now,
especially because each LLM may tokenize differently.

declarations.name: Socket
declarations.docComment:
Wraps `FrontendWebsocketClient` with zzz-specific concerns: a
retryable fire-and-forget send queue (distinct from fuz_app's
request-level durable queue), URL input tracking, and a mapping from
fuz_app's `SocketStatus` onto zzz's `AsyncStatus`. Plain reactive class
— not a Cell. Implements `WebsocketRpcConnection` so it can back
`FrontendWebsocketTransport`; the `request` method is a one-line
delegate to the underlying `FrontendWebsocketClient`, keeping the
pending-request map in one canonical place.

The bespoke heartbeat timer has been retired — fuz_app's
`FrontendWebsocketClient` now ships an activity-aware heartbeat that
sends the shared `heartbeat_action` at `heartbeat_interval` idle.
Assigning `heartbeat_interval` pushes the new policy into the live
client immediately (the timer is restarted in place when connected).

Reconnect settings (`reconnect_delay`, `reconnect_delay_max`,
`auto_reconnect`) propagate to the underlying client on assignment
via `apply_reconnect_policy()` — in-flight waits are monotonically
shortened (never extended).

members.name: last_send_time
members.docComment:
UI timestamps for the "last send" / "last receive" diagnostics in
`CapabilityWebsocket`. Not used for heartbeat scheduling —
fuz_app's client owns that.

members.name: reconnect_attempt
members.docComment:
Changes each time a close fires — used by the UI as an animation key so
each reconnect wait restarts the progress bar.

members.name: request
members.docComment:
Delegate to the underlying `FrontendWebsocketClient.request` — keeps the
pending-request map, durable queue, and `AbortSignal` cancel in one
canonical place. Rejects when there is no client (call `connect()` first).
members.typeSignature: (method: string, params?: unknown, options?: { signal?: AbortSignal | undefined; queue?: boolean | undefined; id?: string | number | undefined; } | undefined): Promise<unknown>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { signal?: AbortSignal | undefined; queue?: boolean | undefined; id?: string | number | undefined; } | undefined

members.name: apply_reconnect_policy
members.docComment:
Push the current `auto_reconnect` / `reconnect_delay` /
`reconnect_delay_max` fields to the underlying client. No-op when
there is no client (next `connect()` picks them up from construction).

members.name: heartbeat_interval
members.docComment:
Heartbeat idle interval in ms. Writing pushes the new policy into the
underlying client immediately — when connected, the live timer is
restarted in place; when disconnected, the policy is stashed for the
next `connect()`.

members.name: reconnect_delay
members.docComment:
Reconnect policy fields. Assignments push into the underlying client via
`apply_reconnect_policy()` so in-flight waits honor the new policy
(monotonically shortened, never extended).

modules.moduleComment:
Test-only action specs and handler.

`_testing_emit_notifications` + `_testing_notification` are kept out of the
production codegen surface (`all_action_specs` in `action_specs.ts`).
They register on the live HTTP-RPC + WebSocket dispatchers only when
`ZZZ_ENABLE_TEST_ACTIONS=1`, which the cross-process test binary
(`testing_zzz_server`) sets unconditionally and production never does.

Naming follows the test-binary safety convention — `_testing_*` /
`Testing*` for anything wire-visible or artifact-shipping, matching
`fuz_testing` / `TestingArgon2idHasher` / `testing_zzz_server`.

declarations.name: Thread
declarations.docComment:
A thread is a linear sequence of turns that maintains a chronological
record of interactions between the user and the AI.

members.name: pending
members.docComment:
Reactive flag: true while `send_message` has an in-flight `completion_create`.
Owned by the thread so multiple views of the same thread stay in sync.
Callers that bypass `send_message` and call `app.api.completion_create`
directly will not update this flag.

Abort the in-flight `completion_create` call (if any). Safe to call when
nothing is pending — no-op. The frontend WS client rejects the pending
promise with `request_cancelled` and fires a `cancel` notification so the
server can stop its provider stream.

declarations.name: Time
declarations.docComment:
Reactive time management class that provides time-related utilities.
Has a configurable update interval that defaults to
a full minute to minimize wasteful reactivity,
so it's suitable for any cases that need at best 1-minute precision, unless reconfigured.

members.name: DEFAULT_INTERVAL
members.docComment:
Default update interval in milliseconds (1 minute).
The idea is to minimize reactivity and CPU usage for a common use case.

members.name: now
members.docComment:
Current time that updates on the configured interval.
This is reactive and can be used in derived computations.

members.name: interval
members.docComment: The interval in milliseconds between time updates.

members.name: running
members.docComment: Whether the interval timer is currently running.

members.name: start
members.docComment: Starts the interval timer if it's not already running.

members.name: stop
members.docComment: Stops the interval timer if it's running.

members.name: restart
members.docComment: Restarts the interval timer with a new interval.

members.name: update_now
members.docComment: Updates the now to the current time immediately.

members.name: destroy
members.docComment: Override `Cell`'s destroy method to ensure timer cleanup.

A workspace represents an open directory that zzz is watching and serving.

modules.moduleComment:
zzz-specific JSON-RPC error codes extending fuz_app's base set.

fuz_app provides 5 standard + 8 general application error codes.
zzz adds domain-specific codes for AI provider errors.
